title: Inside the Food Truck Mafia Wreaking Havoc Around the National Mall
description: "Turf wars. Food and fire hazards. $15 ice-cream cones. How an organized network of unlicensed food trucks took over America's Front Lawn."
hook: DC's food truck underworld runs like organized crime, complete with turf wars and $15 ice cream cones guarding the National Mall.

title: Two Hundred and Fifty
description: “This is America, I thought, it isn’t right to deny an adult—a paying customer—a beer.”
hook: A small moment of American refusal—a woman's quiet insistence on her right to a beer, and why it matters.

title: Why the ‘Cape Fear’ Acid Freakout Demanded a New Set of Visual Tools
description: Cinematographer Celiana Cárdenas talks about changing lenses and aspect ratios to depict the feverish intensity of the Bowden family's bad trip.
hook: How a cinematographer rebuilt her visual language to capture acid hallucination: aspect ratios and lenses as psychological tools.

title: Madonna ‘Confessions II’ Review: Revelations on a Dance Floor
description:
We weren't sure if Madonna could surprise us anymore. Until she did.

The post Madonna ‘Confessions II’ Review: Revelations on a Dance Floor appeared first on
hook: Madonna's new album proves she can still detonate the dance floor—a calculation of restraint and surrender.

title: Interview: Ross McElwee on Refracting the Past in ‘Sherman’s March’ and ‘Remake’
description:
McElwee discusses, among other things, his new film and staring down the loss of his son.

The post Interview: Ross
hook: A documentarian rewrites his own archive, remaking the past to understand it differently. McElwee on obsession and revision.

title: Why Do I Keep Coming Back to Trains?
description:
“Train Corridor” by Weike Wang One fall I took many Amtraks. It was two trips per week or four trains. I was teaching at two different colleges, in two different cities. Most semesters I commute only to one school, so one trip per week or two trains. In total, I have taken hundreds of Amtraks […]

The post Why Do I Keep Coming Back to Trains? appeared first on
hook: Why trains became her obsession: Weike Wang on the geography of splitting yourself between two lives, two cities, one body.

title: On the Mark by Florence Hazrat review – a fascinating history of punctuation
description:
This lavishly researched book shows that dots and dashes are an essential component of style, whether you’re a medieval monk or Donald Trump

How do you feel about exclamation marks? Otherwise known as gaspers, screamers, dog’s cocks, or shrieks. In his Modern English Usage, Fowler said that using too many betrays an “uneducated or unpractised writer”. Martin Amis called them “joke badges”, and Theodor Adorno “soundless cymbal-crashing”. The novelist Elmore Leonard specified that you wer
hook: Punctuation isn't decoration—it's the skeleton of style, from medieval monks to how Trump writes fury into grammar.
